title: Elisa_kits_landing_page
content:
ELISA Kits

Boster ELISA kits offer a comprehensive selection for cytokines, growth factors, hormones, chemokines, and other biomarkers across various species including human, mouse, and rat. Each kit is meticulously developed, validated, and quality-controlled in-house to ensure top performance.

Compare the 5 ELISA Formats View QC & Validation
Limited-time offer: 40% OFF Top 100 best-selling ELISA kits

Why Choose Boster ELISA Kits

Comprehensive, Verified Coverage

Over 1,200 targets across human, mouse, rat, and other species — including cytokines, hormones, growth factors, chemokines, and biomarkers. Boster’s ELISA kits are meticulously developed, validated, and quality-controlled in-house, ensuring traceable performance data beyond mere catalog claims.

This enables you to standardize assays seamlessly across projects and teams, backed by consistent documentation and robust QC practices for reliable results every time.

At a glance

1,200+
Targets validated in-house
Multi-species
Human • Mouse • Rat • More
  • In-house workflow: development → validation → QC
  • Traceable data: performance metrics documented
  • Not aggregated: avoids catalog-only claims

Comprehensive Validation and Quality Assurance

Each ELISA kit batch is evaluated in-house for specificity, linearity, recovery, precision, stability, and lot-to-lot consistency. Precision targets are defined by repeatability and between-lot consistency to support reproducible, publication-grade results.

  • Reproducibility: Rigorous multi-lot testing to minimize variance
  • Precision: Repeatability (Intra-assay CV ≤ 10%) and lot-to-lot consistency (CV ≤ 15%)
  • Linearity: Comprehensive dilution series testing across the dynamic range for accurate quantitation
  • Specificity: Cross-reactivity assessments against related proteins
  • Stability: Long-term performance tracking to verify consistent kit performance
  • Recovery: Spike-and-recovery protocols in relevant matrices to validate accuracy

Typical acceptance targets

Linearity
80–120%
Recovery
80–120%
Precision
Repeatability (Intra-assay CV) ≤ 10%
Lot-to-lot consistency (CV) ≤ 15%
Lot-to-lot consistency is continuously monitored to support reproducible results across projects.

Format Details

Compare ELISA formats with an on-page guide. Expand a format to see best-fit scenarios, what you get, and trade-offs.

PicoKine® ELISA Kits: When should I choose a ready-to-run sandwich ELISA?

Best for: Achieving publication-grade quantitation with a dependable, ready-to-use sandwich ELISA.

Why choose it: Benefit from a standardized workflow that ensures reproducible results across various experiments and batch lots.

What you get: Receive a comprehensive kit configuration with documentation that meets rigorous quality control standards.

Trade-offs: Offers less flexibility compared to EZ-Set and generally requires more time than Quick formats.

Explore PicoKine® ELISA Kits →

Quick ELISA (90 min): When should I prioritize speed?

Best for: Rapid high-throughput screening and urgent experiments demanding quick results.

Why choose it: Efficient workflow tailored for delivering results in approximately 90 minutes, enhancing productivity.

What you get: Accelerated assay procedures with comprehensive, step-by-step instructions for ease of use.

Trade-offs: Limited customization options; opt for High Sensitivity formats for ultra-low detection needs.

Explore Quick ELISA Kits →

High Sensitivity ELISA: When do I need ultra-low-abundance detection?

Best for: Ultra-low-abundance targets, limited sample volume, or complex matrices.

Why choose it: Optimized to improve detection in the low concentration range.

What you get: A comprehensive high-sensitivity kit setup with expert guidance, ensuring alignment with stringent quality control standards.

Trade-offs: Consistent technique is crucial; careful sample preparation and precise dilution strategies are essential.

Explore High Sensitivity ELISA Kits →

EZ-Set Antibody Pairs: When should I build or customize my own ELISA?

Best for: Experienced ELISA users seeking flexibility, customization, or exploratory assay development.

Why choose it: Offers core assay “building blocks” allowing you to customize plates, incubation, and workflow.

What you get: Matched antibody pair and standards/core reagents tailored to your EZ-Set configuration.

Trade-offs: Involves development and optimization efforts, but provides maximum control.

Explore EZ-Set Antibody Pairs →

Multiplex ELISA: When do I need panels or pathway profiling?

Best for: Simultaneously measuring multiple biomarkers in a single sample, especially useful for cytokine panels and pathway-related proteins.

Why choose it: Enhances efficiency and data richness when sample volume is limited, yet multiple target analysis is necessary.

What you get: A comprehensive panel-based workflow along with expert guidance on expected readout results.

Trade-offs: Increased workflow complexity; ensure confirmation of readout setup requirements prior to ordering.

Explore Multiplex ELISA Kits →

Testimonials

What researchers say about Boster ELISA kits.

I used the ELISA kit to detect the BDNF level in several samples, including brain tissues (cortex and hippocampus), cultured astrocyte (cell lysis) and astrocyte conditioned medium. The kit works very well for cell lysis and brain tissues according to the instruction. For the cell medium, I suggest that users need modify the protocol a little bit, such as prolong the incubation time for samples in the wells for overnight. The kit has a good quality.

Kit & SKU: Mouse BDNF ELISA Kit PicoKine® (EK0309)

View Product →

Job Position: Research Associate

University/Organization: University of Toronto

The instructions are clear, concise, and the process is more streamlined than other kits. I highly recommend investing in these kits compared to other, less expensive ones.

Kit & SKU: Human MMP-9 ELISA Kit PicoKine® (EK0465)

University/Organization:
University of Louisville
Microbiology & immunology

I used the kit to test the ADAMTS1 protein level in human endothelial cell lysates. The instructions are clear and the kit worked the first time with reproducible results. I will recommend this product to my colleagues.

Kit & SKU: Human ADAMTS1 ELISA Kit PicoKine® (EK1340)

View Product →

Huiyuan Tang

Job Position: Lab Specialist

University/Organization: University of Utah

I am very much satisfied with the product Human IL-1 Beta PicoKine™ ELISA Kit (EK0392) purchased from Boster Bio. I used the Boster Bio ElISA kit for the first time and I am very happy with this product. After trying the IL-1beta kit, I have purchased two other cytokine detection ELISA kits from Boster Bio. The methodology of Human IL-1 Beta PicoKine™ ELISA Kit is simplified and user friendly. It required minimum optimization for my experiments. The written instructions provided with the kit is very useful for running the ELISA. The results obtained so far have been consistent and reproducible. For my future ELISA experiments, I'll be buying the ELISA kit from Boster Bio.

Kit & SKU: Human IL-1 Beta/IL-1F2/IL1B ELISA Kit PicoKine® (EK0392)

FAQs

Questions about data reliability, validation, and Boster’s quality guarantee for ELISA kits.

Q1. How do I know Boster ELISA kits deliver real, reproducible data?

Boster ELISA kits are meticulously developed, validated, and quality-controlled in-house. Each batch undergoes rigorous testing for critical performance metrics, including specificity, linearity, recovery, precision, stability, and lot-to-lot consistency. This ensures that our kits deliver reliable and traceable performance, perfectly aligned with datasheet specifications.

Q2. What does the Boster Quality Guarantee cover for ELISA kits?

We assure that our ELISA kits adhere to the exact specifications detailed on our datasheets and are rigorously tested before shipping. Should a kit not perform as expected for its specified application or reactivity, we commit to a thorough investigation and will provide a refund or free replacement in accordance with our guarantee terms. Your satisfaction and trust in our products are our top priorities.

Q3. What happens if I run into problems during my ELISA experiment?

If you encounter an issue, our technical support team will respond with personalized troubleshooting advice within 24 hours. We’ll help you identify likely root causes (e.g., sample handling, incubation/wash conditions, matrix effects) and recommend corrective actions.

Q4. If support can’t make my experiment work, what are my options?

If we can’t resolve the problem and make your experiment work, we will provide a full refund or a free replacement, in line with the Boster Quality Guarantee. Full terms and conditions are provided in the guarantee section below.

Support

Need help or have questions?
